title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability is a severe 10% (36/192), B200 is tight at 14% (102/191), A100 sits at 20% (140/334), and H200 at 21% (49/185). H100 spot surged 42.8% day-over-day to $2.86/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling a demand shock likely driven by utilization spiking to 90.3%, up 10.5 points in 30 days.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability is a severe 10% (36/192), B200 is tight at 14% (102/191), A100 sits at 20% (140/334), and H200 at 21% (49/185). H100 spot surged 42.8% day-over-day to $2.86/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack, signaling a demand shock likely driven by utilization spiking to 90.3%, up 10.5 points in 30 days.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs $10.44/M tokens for Llama 70B, meaning any provider pricing output at or below $6/M on comparable models is burning cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling at catastrophic losses, subsidizing adoption. OpenAI grades Neutral on margins, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at $3.66/M raw inference cost is the most margin-efficient accelerator in the stack and undervalued given 14% availability and rising utilization. H200 is overvalued at $3.88/hr spot with 121.7% annualized volatility and declining utilization, down 4.5 points in 30 days — rotate H200 exposure into B200 contracts.
bullets: 10% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply of any class with only 36 of 192 units free.
14% of B200 GPUs are available, marking severe shortage territory at 102 of 191 units.
$2.86/hr H100 spot is up 42.8% in a single day, the largest move across all GPU classes.
121.7% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute asset to hold.
$0.06 per million output tokens is the floor price, set by Meta Llama 3.1 8B and Google Gemma 2 2B.
21.76 is the LLMflation Index, meaning frontier model pricing has fallen roughly 78% since GPT-4 launch.
